feedback: The build-detail-native-logs-widget implementation is complete and meets all acceptance criteria. The iframe section has been properly replaced with a native Blazor logs widget. All required CSS classes are defined and properly applied. The widget correctly uses _job.Logs field and the ILogParser.ParseLogMessages() method for parsing. Empty state messaging is present for null/empty log scenarios. DownloadLogs method correctly uses JSRuntime.InvokeVoidAsync for JavaScript execution. The implementation matches existing Build Log container styling with proper scrollable container and max-height. While tests exist for ILogParser with good coverage, they could not be executed directly due to .NET SDK version mismatch in the environment.

iteration_history.summary: The code implementation has a BLOCKING defect: ILogParser.ParseLogMessages() is called but ILogParser is never injected, causing a NullReferenceException at runtime. BuildDetail.razor references LogParser.ParseLogMessages(_job.Logs) but no @inject ILogParser LogParser exists in the page directives. Also, the DownloadLogs method generates JavaScript but doesn't execute it. Missing CSS classes (.build-log-container, .build-log-timestamp, .build-log-content, .build-log-empty, .build-log-cached, .build-log-actions) are not defined in site.css, causing unstyled elements.

The build-detail-native-logs-widget implementation is complete and meets all acceptance criteria. The iframe section has been properly replaced with a native Blazor logs widget. All required CSS classes are defined and properly applied. The widget correctly uses _job.Logs field and the ILogParser.ParseLogMessages() method for parsing. Empty state messaging is present for null/empty log scenarios. DownloadLogs method correctly uses JSRuntime.InvokeVoidAsync for JavaScript execution. The implementation matches existing Build Log container styling with proper scrollable container and max-height. While tests exist for ILogParser with good coverage, they could not be executed directly due to .NET SDK version mismatch in the environment.
